title: The AI Bias Codex
subtitle: A data-first map of the biases and failure patterns that surface when people interact with AI systems.

statements.label: We notice what the interface highlights, ranks, and repeats

leaves.statement: We notice what the interface highlights, ranks, and repeats
leaves.one_sentence_definition: The tendency to notice some cues more readily than competing cues.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Ranked outputs, highlighted answers, and suggestion chips steer user attention toward selected cues first.

leaves.statement: We notice what the interface highlights, ranks, and repeats
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People judge importance and likelihood by what comes to mind most easily.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI surfaces the most retrievable answer first, making repeated patterns feel more representative than they are.

leaves.statement: We notice what the interface highlights, ranks, and repeats
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Repeated exposure increases familiarity and can increase preference or acceptance.
leaves.interaction_rationale: When AI tools restate patterns or reuse familiar framings, users can start preferring them for that reason alone.

leaves.statement: We notice what the interface highlights, ranks, and repeats
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Repeated claims feel truer even when repetition adds no evidence.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI-generated summaries, recommendations, and answer restatements can convert repetition into perceived truth.

statements.label: We trust outputs that sound fluent, certain, and complete

leaves.statement: We trust outputs that sound fluent, certain, and complete
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Information that is easier to process feels more familiar, credible, and right.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Cleanly worded AI output often feels reliable before users inspect whether the content is actually grounded.

leaves.statement: We trust outputs that sound fluent, certain, and complete
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Claims can feel true because they sound right or fit intuition, not because they are verified.
leaves.interaction_rationale: LLMs produce polished explanations that often reward intuitive plausibility more than evidential rigor.

leaves.statement: We trust outputs that sound fluent, certain, and complete
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can feel unwarranted confidence in judgments that rest on weak evidence.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Detailed AI answers can create a false sense that the model's internal case is stronger than it is.

leaves.statement: We trust outputs that sound fluent, certain, and complete
leaves.one_sentence_definition: AI systems sometimes present false or unsupported content as if it were factual.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users often take confident, well-formed answers at face value and miss that the model is fabricating.

statements.label: We give extra weight to vivid, personalized, or emotional examples

leaves.statement: We give extra weight to vivid, personalized, or emotional examples
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A striking example can outweigh broader evidence in judgment.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI assistants make evidence feel concrete by generating custom stories and examples on demand.

leaves.statement: We give extra weight to vivid, personalized, or emotional examples
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People respond more strongly to specific, vivid individuals than to abstract groups.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI-generated personas and case studies can make one synthetic example outweigh population-level evidence.

leaves.statement: We give extra weight to vivid, personalized, or emotional examples
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Emotional reactions become shortcuts for judging risk, value, and credibility.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI outputs with emotionally loaded wording can steer user judgment before analytic checking begins.

leaves.statement: We give extra weight to vivid, personalized, or emotional examples
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Negative information often has stronger impact than equally strong positive information.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Warnings, failure cases, and alarming AI examples can dominate recall and shape trust disproportionally.

statements.label: Human-like cues make systems feel more credible than they are

leaves.statement: Human-like cues make systems feel more credible than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People attribute human-like feelings, motives, and traits to AI systems.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Voice, tone, and conversational polish invite users to overread agency and competence into the system.

leaves.statement: Human-like cues make systems feel more credible than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can attribute far more understanding to a conversational program than it warrants.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Even simple back-and-forth can make users feel the model understands them as a social partner.

leaves.statement: Human-like cues make systems feel more credible than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People often apply human social scripts to computers and software agents automatically.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI assistants trigger politeness, trust, reciprocity, and role assumptions that change how output is judged.

leaves.statement: Human-like cues make systems feel more credible than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Interfaces can create a stronger sense that another social presence is really there.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Chat, voice, avatar, and streaming cues make AI feel socially present and therefore easier to trust.

statements.label: Recommendations and defaults narrow what we explore and question

leaves.statement: Recommendations and defaults narrow what we explore and question
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Personalized curation can isolate people inside a narrower information environment.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI ranking and recommendation systems quietly reduce exposure to disconfirming material.

leaves.statement: Recommendations and defaults narrow what we explore and question
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Beliefs get reinforced when similar views circulate inside a closed loop.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI feeds and social recommendation systems can keep users inside self-confirming content loops.

leaves.statement: Recommendations and defaults narrow what we explore and question
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People disproportionately stick with preselected or default options.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Suggested prompts, one-click accepts, and default model settings steer users without much reflection.

leaves.statement: Recommendations and defaults narrow what we explore and question
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Recommendation engines sort and promote a subset of available options or content.
leaves.interaction_rationale: What the system recommends becomes the local reality users inspect, compare, and remember.

statements.label: We infer understanding from language that only imitates understanding

leaves.statement: We infer understanding from language that only imitates understanding
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A system can manipulate symbols convincingly without genuine understanding.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Fluent dialogue makes users forget that token-level behavior is not the same thing as comprehension.

leaves.statement: We infer understanding from language that only imitates understanding
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Large language models can mimic text patterns without grounded semantic understanding.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users often read deep understanding into outputs that are statistically assembled from prior language patterns.

leaves.statement: We infer understanding from language that only imitates understanding
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Symbols do not automatically connect to real-world meaning just because they are manipulated coherently.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI can produce the right-looking words without anchoring them in the world users assume it knows.

leaves.statement: We infer understanding from language that only imitates understanding
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Human-like conversation can be mistaken for intelligence or understanding.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Passing a conversational threshold makes users upgrade their beliefs about model competence too far.

statements.label: We treat confident presentation as evidence of truth

leaves.statement: We treat confident presentation as evidence of truth
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People overweight claims that seem to come from an authority.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI systems inherit borrowed authority from branding, interface design, and technical mystique.

leaves.statement: We treat confident presentation as evidence of truth
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A strong impression in one dimension spills over into other judgments.
leaves.interaction_rationale: If an AI sounds competent or helpful, users may infer it is also accurate, fair, or thoughtful.

leaves.statement: We treat confident presentation as evidence of truth
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A claim is treated as stronger because of who appears to support it.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users can substitute the model's perceived status for direct evidence about the answer itself.

leaves.statement: We treat confident presentation as evidence of truth
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People infer higher value or truth from what is easier to read and process.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Smoothly presented AI claims feel more trustworthy than slower, less polished alternatives.

statements.label: We fill opaque systems with stories about intent, memory, and fairness

leaves.statement: We fill opaque systems with stories about intent, memory, and fairness
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Opaque systems invite judgments without exposing the internal process that produced them.
leaves.interaction_rationale: When users cannot inspect the mechanism, they fill gaps with stories that may not match the system.

leaves.statement: We fill opaque systems with stories about intent, memory, and fairness
leaves.one_sentence_definition: XAI aims to make AI outputs and decisions more understandable to humans.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users often take any explanation interface as proof that they now understand the model well enough.

leaves.statement: We fill opaque systems with stories about intent, memory, and fairness
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People reason through simplified internal models of how a system works.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Human-AI interaction depends on partial mental models that are often good enough to use, but still wrong.

leaves.statement: We fill opaque systems with stories about intent, memory, and fairness
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People predict behavior by treating an entity as if it had beliefs and desires.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users routinely explain AI outputs by imagining goals and intentions the model does not literally have.

statements.label: We project our own assumptions into ambiguous prompts and outputs

leaves.statement: We project our own assumptions into ambiguous prompts and outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People favor information that confirms their prior beliefs and expectations.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users often judge AI answers by whether they support the story they already wanted to hear.

leaves.statement: We project our own assumptions into ambiguous prompts and outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Current preferences and states get projected onto future or ambiguous judgments.
leaves.interaction_rationale: People read their own intent and context into underspecified prompts and then overcredit the model.

leaves.statement: We project our own assumptions into ambiguous prompts and outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People assume their own interpretation is the obvious, objective one.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users often mistake their reading of an AI answer for the answer's only reasonable meaning.

leaves.statement: We project our own assumptions into ambiguous prompts and outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People project features of their own mind onto the world and treat them as objective.
leaves.interaction_rationale: In AI use, users can confuse their own framing assumptions with properties of the model itself.

statements.label: We mistake pattern-matching and agreement for reasoning and explanation

leaves.statement: We mistake pattern-matching and agreement for reasoning and explanation
leaves.one_sentence_definition: A system can affirm the user's stance instead of independently tracking what is true.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Agreement is easily mistaken for insight when the model mirrors the user's assumptions fluently.

leaves.statement: We mistake pattern-matching and agreement for reasoning and explanation
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Gaps can be filled with coherent but false explanations that feel internally plausible.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI systems often invent seamless causal stories, and users mistake narrative smoothness for reasoning.

leaves.statement: We mistake pattern-matching and agreement for reasoning and explanation
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People perceive a meaningful relationship between variables that are not truly linked.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users can overread model associations or co-occurring phrases as evidence of deep causal structure.

leaves.statement: We mistake pattern-matching and agreement for reasoning and explanation
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People detect meaningful patterns, forms, or signals in ambiguous noise.
leaves.interaction_rationale: In human-AI interaction, users can see intention or structured reasoning where there is only surface patterning.

statements.label: We defer to AI when it feels objective, authoritative, or efficient

leaves.statement: We defer to AI when it feels objective, authoritative, or efficient
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People over-favor automated suggestions and underweight conflicting human or contextual evidence.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI advice often looks systematic and objective, which makes it easier to obey than to audit.

leaves.statement: We defer to AI when it feels objective, authoritative, or efficient
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People sometimes prefer algorithmic judgment over human judgment because it appears more objective.
leaves.interaction_rationale: In some domains, AI recommendations gain extra persuasive force simply by being machine-generated.

leaves.statement: We defer to AI when it feels objective, authoritative, or efficient
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Automation can be used or trusted in situations where closer human monitoring is still required.
leaves.interaction_rationale: People treat AI output as a final answer instead of one input that still needs context and review.

leaves.statement: We defer to AI when it feels objective, authoritative, or efficient
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Complex social problems get reframed as problems that better technology alone can solve.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI can look like a clean technical substitute for judgment, policy, and institutional accountability.

statements.label: We reject AI when it threatens agency, expertise, or accountability

leaves.statement: We reject AI when it threatens agency, expertise, or accountability
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can reject algorithmic advice more harshly than equivalent human advice.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI recommendations are often resisted when errors feel less forgivable or accountability feels unclear.

leaves.statement: We reject AI when it threatens agency, expertise, or accountability
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People push back when they feel their freedom or control is being constrained.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Highly directive AI suggestions can provoke resistance simply because they feel autonomy-reducing.

leaves.statement: We reject AI when it threatens agency, expertise, or accountability
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Reliance on technology can erode human skill, confidence, and judgment over time.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users may resist AI when they see it hollowing out the expertise needed to supervise it well.

leaves.statement: We reject AI when it threatens agency, expertise, or accountability
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can discount useful ideas because they originate from outside their own group or practice.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Teams may resist AI help because it feels external, alien, or disrespectful to local expertise.

statements.label: Once the system seems reliable, we stop monitoring it closely

leaves.statement: Once the system seems reliable, we stop monitoring it closely
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Automation can push humans into passive monitoring roles that degrade manual oversight.
leaves.interaction_rationale: The better the AI appears, the less prepared users become to catch or recover from failure.

leaves.statement: Once the system seems reliable, we stop monitoring it closely
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Perceived reliability reduces vigilance and can make automation failures harder to notice.
leaves.interaction_rationale: As trust in AI rises, users may stop checking assumptions, edge cases, or missing evidence.

leaves.statement: Once the system seems reliable, we stop monitoring it closely
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Effective supervision depends on maintaining an accurate grasp of what is happening now.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI can narrow situational awareness when the user only sees the model's summary instead of the raw state.

leaves.statement: Once the system seems reliable, we stop monitoring it closely
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Sustained monitoring degrades when attention must be maintained over long, repetitive periods.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Supervising AI is often boring when nothing seems wrong, which makes misses more likely when it is.

statements.label: We choose what is easiest to rank, compare, or automate

leaves.statement: We choose what is easiest to rank, compare, or automate
leaves.one_sentence_definition: When a measure becomes a target, it stops being a reliable measure.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI systems often optimize what is legible and measurable rather than what humans actually value.

leaves.statement: We choose what is easiest to rank, compare, or automate
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Quantifiable data can crowd out important factors that are harder to measure.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI decision pipelines privilege measurable proxies and hide what they leave out.

leaves.statement: We choose what is easiest to rank, compare, or automate
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People search where information is easiest to find rather than where the answer really is.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI encourages decision-making around structured, available inputs instead of missing but important context.

leaves.statement: We choose what is easiest to rank, compare, or automate
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Available tools can distort how problems are framed and which solutions seem natural.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Once AI is in the toolbox, people start reshaping tasks to fit what the model can score or generate.

statements.label: We keep following AI-shaped workflows after investing time, trust, and habit

leaves.statement: We keep following AI-shaped workflows after investing time, trust, and habit
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Past investment keeps people committed even when future value is weak.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Once teams spend time tuning prompts and workflows, they keep using them longer than warranted.

leaves.statement: We keep following AI-shaped workflows after investing time, trust, and habit
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Existing options gain extra inertia simply because they are already in place.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Once AI is embedded in workflow defaults, reverting to manual judgment starts to feel abnormal.

leaves.statement: We keep following AI-shaped workflows after investing time, trust, and habit
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People double down on a chosen course even after warning signs accumulate.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Organizations can keep expanding AI use after visible failures because reversing course is reputationally costly.

leaves.statement: We keep following AI-shaped workflows after investing time, trust, and habit
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Early adoption can make later alternatives less attractive or harder to switch to.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Tooling, data formats, habits, and vendor integration make AI choices sticky long after doubts appear.

statements.label: Repeated AI framings become familiar and start to feel true

leaves.statement: Repeated AI framings become familiar and start to feel true
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Familiar options and narratives are favored over unfamiliar ones.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Repeated AI framings become easier to retrieve, which makes them feel more credible and usable.

leaves.statement: Repeated AI framings become familiar and start to feel true
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Repeated public circulation can make an idea feel widely accepted and therefore more true.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI-generated repetition can accelerate cascades by amplifying the same phrasing across many contexts.

leaves.statement: Repeated AI framings become familiar and start to feel true
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Different wording of the same information can change judgment and choice.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI reframes the same facts in multiple persuasive ways, which changes how users perceive them.

leaves.statement: Repeated AI framings become familiar and start to feel true
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can accept repeated suggestions and fold them into belief or memory.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Persistent AI prompts, corrections, and recommendations can shift what users come to accept as reasonable.

statements.label: Personalized systems feed us more of what we already attend to

leaves.statement: Personalized systems feed us more of what we already attend to
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People seek information that fits what they already believe and avoid conflict.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI personalization compounds selective exposure by learning and replaying prior preferences.

leaves.statement: Personalized systems feed us more of what we already attend to
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People interpret ambiguous information in ways that fit prior beliefs.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Even when AI offers mixed evidence, users often notice the parts that confirm their current stance.

leaves.statement: Personalized systems feed us more of what we already attend to
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Similarity drives connection, attention, and information flow between people and groups.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI personalization often learns from like-with-like behavior and serves more of the same back.

leaves.statement: Personalized systems feed us more of what we already attend to
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Data-driven targeting customizes messages for people with specific inferred traits.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI-optimized targeting reinforces what users already click, fear, desire, or identify with.

statements.label: AI can launder stereotypes into neutral-seeming outputs

leaves.statement: AI can launder stereotypes into neutral-seeming outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Algorithms can reproduce or amplify patterned unfairness in decisions and representations.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users may treat biased model outputs as neutral because they arrive through technical systems.

leaves.statement: AI can launder stereotypes into neutral-seeming outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Fairness research studies how to evaluate and mitigate unequal outcomes in machine learning.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Fairness language can obscure how much value judgment still remains in what the system outputs.

leaves.statement: AI can launder stereotypes into neutral-seeming outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Generalized beliefs about groups compress people into oversimplified expected traits.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI outputs can restate group stereotypes in polished language that sounds detached and objective.

leaves.statement: AI can launder stereotypes into neutral-seeming outputs
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People recognize bias more easily in others than in themselves or their tools.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Users often scrutinize human bias while overlooking the biases embedded in AI outputs they rely on.

statements.label: Shared AI outputs can make views look more common and legitimate than they are

leaves.statement: Shared AI outputs can make views look more common and legitimate than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People use others' apparent choices as evidence for what is correct or acceptable.
leaves.interaction_rationale: When AI content is reused everywhere, frequency itself starts to signal legitimacy.

leaves.statement: Shared AI outputs can make views look more common and legitimate than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Perceived popularity can make positions more attractive simply because they seem popular.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Widely copied AI framings can create the sense that everyone already agrees with them.

leaves.statement: Shared AI outputs can make views look more common and legitimate than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can copy others' choices while ignoring their own private information.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Once AI-generated claims are widely repeated, users infer consensus from repetition rather than evidence.

leaves.statement: Shared AI outputs can make views look more common and legitimate than they are
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Pressure toward consensus can suppress dissent and reduce critical evaluation.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Shared AI summaries can harden group consensus by giving everyone the same polished starting point.

statements.label: We remember the answer and forget the uncertainty, caveats, and provenance

leaves.statement: We remember the answer and forget the uncertainty, caveats, and provenance
leaves.one_sentence_definition: Later information can distort how an earlier event or claim is remembered.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI paraphrases and restatements can overwrite a user's memory of what the source originally said.

leaves.statement: We remember the answer and forget the uncertainty, caveats, and provenance
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People can remember content while misremembering where it came from.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI-generated text is easy to remember and easy to detach from whether it was verified, guessed, or cited.

leaves.statement: We remember the answer and forget the uncertainty, caveats, and provenance
leaves.one_sentence_definition: After the fact, outcomes feel more predictable and obvious than they really were.
leaves.interaction_rationale: Once an AI explanation is seen, users may misremember their own earlier uncertainty or competing views.

leaves.statement: We remember the answer and forget the uncertainty, caveats, and provenance
leaves.one_sentence_definition: People remember where information can be retrieved instead of encoding the information itself.
leaves.interaction_rationale: AI assistants intensify offloading by making users remember the tool rather than the justification.
